qas.q: The name and EPA registration numbers of the shippers, carriers,and destination must appear on
qas.a: Hazardous Waste Manifest

qas.q: What is a placard?
qas.a: Diamond shaped warning signs placed on vehicles

qas.q: When carrying class 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives, you must
qas.a: have a written route plan

qas.q: The hazardous materials regulations are intended to do what?
qas.a: To contain material, communicate the risk, and assure safe drivers and equipment

qas.q: When transporting hazardous materials, which of the following is NOT a responsibility of the driver?
qas.a: Driver prepares the shipping papers and supplies the placards

qas.q: Which of the following indicates the order in which information must be shown in the description area of a shipping paper?
qas.a: Proper shipping name, hazard class, ID number, packing group

qas.q: Diamond shaped warning labels must be placed on hazardous materials packages. If the package cannot accommodate a label
qas.a: The label must be put on a tag that is securely attached to the package

qas.q: Where should placards be displayed if you are required to use them?
qas.a: On both ends and both sides of the vehicle

qas.q: If a W appears in column 1 of the Hazardous Materials Table, What does it indicate about the hazardous material?
qas.a: Only applies when the material is being transported by water

qas.q: How is the Hazardous Materials Table ordered?
qas.a: Alphabetical order by proper shipping name

qas.q: What placards, if any, do you need if your load includes 20 lbs of Division 2.3 gas and 1,0001 lbs of flammable gas
qas.a: None, you cannot load these materials together

qas.q: For Division 1.1 or 1.2 Explosives A, should the floor liner required to be stainless steel?
qas.a: No

qas.q: A hazard class that uses transport indexes to determine the amount that can be loaded in a single vehicle is
qas.a: class 7 radioactive materials

qas.q: The Emergency Response Guide, ERG, can best be explained as
qas.a: A guide for firefighters, police, and industry workers on how to protect themselves and the public from hazardous materials AND a guidebook for truck drivers, dock workers, and shippers to protect themselves

qas.q: Which of the following is a reason that shippers package materials
qas.a: contain

qas.q: It is very important that you never smoke around which hazard classes
qas.a: explosives, flammable gas, oxidizers, flammable solids. flammable liquids

qas.q: There must be a hazardous materials identification number of on the _______ and on the _____
qas.a: shipping papers; package

qas.q: When is it acceptable for non-hazardous materials to be described by hazard class words or identification numbers
qas.a: never

qas.q: How often should you check the tires if your placarded trailer has dual tires?
qas.a: Every 2 hours or 100 miles

qas.q: With Division 1.2 or 1.3 Explosive B, how close to the traveled part of the roadway can you park
qas.a: minimum of 5 feet

qas.q: Where can you find a shipper's certification?
qas.a: On the original shipping paper

qas.q: A portable tank differs from a cargo tank in that
qas.a: portable tanks are bulk containers, which are not permanently attached to a vehicle

qas.q: Do you need to stop before a railroad crossing if you are hauling 100 lbs of Division 4.3 Dangerous when wet material?
qas.a: Yes

qas.q: Cargo tanks ___
qas.a: are bulk packaging permanently attached to a vehicle

qas.q: Five hazard classes that require placarding in any amount are__
qas.a: poison gas 2.3, dangerous when wet 4.3, explosives 1.3, explosives 1.2, explosives 1.1

qas.q: You must keep shipping papers describing hazardous materials___
qas.a: in a pouch on the driver's door or in a clear view within immediate reach while the seat belt is fastened while driving on the driver's seat when out of the vehicle

qas.q: Drivers placard their vehicle to ___ the risk
qas.a: communicate

qas.q: With Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 Class A or B, how close can you park to a bridge, tunnel, or building?
qas.a: 300 feet

qas.q: Placards vehicles must carry which type of fire extinguisher? UL rating
qas.a: 10 B-C or more

qas.q: A shippers certification is best described as
qas.a: A certification that the package has been prepared according to the rules when the shipper packages hazardous materials

qas.q: What are the 3 hazard classes should not be loaded into a trailer that has a heated air conditioner unit>
qas.a: Class 1 explosives, class 3 flammable liquids, and division 2.1 flammable gas

qas.q: If your engine runs a pump used during delivery of a compressed gas, should you turn off the engine before or after unhooking hoses after delivery>
qas.a: Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose

qas.q: A safe haven is ___
qas.a: an approved place for parking unattended vehicles with explosives

qas.q: A shipment described on the hazardous waste manifest may only be delivered to another____ carrier or treatment facility, who then signs the _____, giving you a copy which you must keep
qas.a: registered, uniform hazardous waste manifest

qas.q: To decide which placards you need, if any, what three things do you need to know?
qas.a: Materials hazard class, amount being shipped,and amount of all hazardous materials of all classes on your vehicle

qas.q: What should you do in the following scenario: at a rest area, you discover your hazardous material shipment is slowly leaking from the vehicle. There is no phone around.....
qas.a: Park it, secure the area, stay there, and send someone for help

qas.q: Which of the following statements about loading and unloading cargo is false?
qas.a: The person in attendance must be within 50 ft of the tank

qas.q: Some hazardous materials may not be loaded together with other hazardous materials. The prohibited combinations are listed in the....
qas.a: segregation and separation chart

qas.q: A vehicle placarded for Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives....
qas.a: may be left unattended in a safe haven

qas.q: If you are hauling hazardous materials and have ____ tires on your vehicle, you must stop and check them every time you park
qas.a: dual

qas.q: If you are required to stop at railroad crossing you must
qas.a: stop 15 to 50 ft before the nearest rail; remember to never shift gears while crossing tracks; proceed only when you are sure no train is coming

qas.q: A "transport index" relates to products that are..
qas.a: radioactive

qas.q: You must have placards for explosives, poisonous gas or flammables exceeding....
qas.a: any amoung

qas.q: The floor lining for explosives may not contain
qas.a: steel or iron; nails or screws; cracks or holes

qas.q: The shipping papers for hazardous materials can be found
qas.a: in a pouch on the driver's door, in a clear view within reach while driving, on the driver's seat when out of the vehicle

qas.q: You may use DANGEROUS placards instead of separate placards for each Table 2 hazard class when:
qas.a: you have 1,001 lbs or more of two or more table 2 hazard classes AND you have not loaded 2,205 lbs or more of any Table 2 hazard class materials at any one place

qas.q: You should turn off your engine when loading flammable liquids unless
qas.a: You need the engine to run the pump

qas.q: On the Hazardous Materials Table, column 1 shows...
qas.a: to what shipping mode the information on that line applies

qas.q: When transporting both hazardous and non hazardous materials, your bill must...
qas.a: Describe the hazardous materials first, have the hazardous materials highlighted or identify the materials by the letter X or RQ in the HM column

qas.q: of the following hazardous material classes, which is most dangerous
You must control how far packages labeled "Radioactive II" or "Radioactive III" are located from people and cargo space walls by referring to....
qas.a: Transport Index and Separation Distances Table

qas.q: A placarded vehicle must have at least ___ placards
When transporting hazardous waste, you must
qas.a: have the word "waste" before the name of the material on the bills, sign a uniform hazardous waste manifest, carry a uniform hazardous waste manifest in the truck

qas.q: The designation RQ identifies the materials...
qas.a: re portable quantity

qas.q: Tankers used to transport hazardous materials on vehicles must be marked within
qas.a: The UN or NA ID number of the product

qas.q: Which of the following statements about radioactive labels is true
qas.a: Yellow III indicates the highest level of radioactivity

qas.q: A safe haven is defined as...
qas.a: a government approved place for parking vehicles loaded with explosives

qas.q: Containment rules are rules that...
qas.a: instruct the driver in loading hazardous materials, instruct the driver in transporting hazardous materials, instruct the driver in unloading bulk tanks hauling hazardous materials

qas.q: The product I markings on a tank must be..
qas.a: black 4" numbers on orange panels

qas.q: You must carry a gas mask with you when transporting....
qas.a: chlorine

qas.q: If the hazardous materials are classified as Forbidden you should...
qas.a: Refuse the load

qas.q: Check placarded vehicles with dual tires at the start of each trip and
qas.a: each time you stop

qas.q: In order to transport hazardous materials which require placards...
qas.a: you must have a commercial drivers license, you must pass a written test on hazardous materials, and you must have the hazardous materials endorsement on your cdl

qas.q: You must notify ____ of any spills exceeding the RQ
qas.a: DOT and EPA

qas.q: When transporting explosives you may not...
qas.a: park within 5 ft of any traveled part of the road; park within 300 ft of a tunnel, park near an open fire

qas.q: Who is responsible for certifying on the bills of lading that the hazardous materials are properly prepated
qas.a: the shipper

qas.q: When the words " inhalation hazard" appear on the shipping papers, you need....
qas.a: placards designating poison

qas.q: There are three lists that drivers, shippers and carriers use to find out if a material is regulated as hazardous. Which one of these choices in not?
qas.a: EPA dangerous materials list

qas.q: When transporting compressed gases, you must.....
qas.a: secure the cylinders in boxes or racks, or brace them down flat

qas.q: Drivers of placard vehicles transporting hazardous materials must stop ___ to ___ feet from the nearest rail of a railroad
qas.a: 15 to 50 ft

qas.q: Special requirements for hazardous materials shipping papers include...
qas.a: required drivers to keep papers either tabbed or on top of other papers

qas.q: Do not smoke within ___ feet of a placarded cargo tank used for class 3 (flammable liquids) or Division 2.1 (gases)
qas.a: 25 ft

qas.q: When transporting explosives, flammable liquid or flammable gas you may not...
qas.a: use cargo heaters

qas.q: The hazard class of all materials specifies...
qas.a: the risks associated with the materials

qas.q: You do not have a hazardous materials endorsement on your commercial driver license. When can you legally haul hazardous materials?
qas.a: only when the vehicle does not require placards

qas.q: When a substance in the materials table is written in italics it means:
qas.a: it is not the proper shipping name. Only those in roman type above it are proper shipping name

qas.q: How many different hazard classes are there?
When hauling explosives 1.1, 1.3 which placard do you use?
qas.a: Explosive 1.1

qas.q: A driver that hauls hazardous materials must attend training every
qas.a: 3 years

qas.q: The basic description of a hazardous materials includes the hazard class, the id number, and the proper shipping name. Which one must appear first on the shipping paper
qas.a: Identification number

qas.q: An A/W the first column of the hazardous materials table means
qas.a: The material is only restricted when shipped by air and/or water

qas.q: When fueling a placarded vehicle, someone must always be
qas.a: At the nozzle, controlling the fuel flow

qas.q: In column 1 the I symbol means
qas.a: international transportation

qas.q: With most hazardous materials, you may park within 5 feet of the road briefly. if work requires it. The materials that are the exception and do not allow parking this close to the road are
qas.a: Explosive A and B

qas.q: The basic shipment of a hazardous product must include....
qas.a: The proper shipping name, the hazard class and identification number

qas.q: The shipper must attach a diamond-shaped label to hazardous materials by....
qas.a: putting a label on the package, putting a label on a tag attached to the product, or putting a label on the product

qas.q: On hazardous Materials Table, proper shipping names are shown....
qas.a: in plain type and in alphabetical order

qas.q: In the event of an accident involving hazardous materials, you should first
qas.a: send someone for help and warn others of danger

qas.q: The hazardous material's identification number is important because.....
qas.a: the shipper must include it on the package, police use the number to identify the material after an accident, the shipper must include it on the shipping paper

qas.q: Product ID numbers must appear on each side and both ends of a portable tank or other bulk packaging containing...
qas.a: hazardous materials 1000 or more

qas.q: Which one of the following descriptions is in the proper order....
qas.a: UN1076, Phosgene, 2.3

qas.q: You should never transport a package labeled poison if you are hauling...
qas.a: food products

qas.q: Entries in the hazardous Materials Table are shown....
qas.a: In alphabetical order by proper shipping names

qas.q: If you are in an accident involving hazardous materials, you should
qas.a: keep all people far away and upwind of the accident

qas.q: What type of shape and size of a placard?
qas.a: 10 3/4 inch square or diamond

qas.q: The placard should be placed on the vehicle....
qas.a: before you drive it

qas.q: The hazardous waste manifest should be
qas.a: tabbed differently and kept on top of all other paperwork

qas.q: A placard must be placed...
qas.a: at least three inches away from any other marking

qas.q: Whenever your vehicle is placarded, do not drive near open fires unless you
qas.a: can safely pass the fire without stopping

qas.q: You have loaded a hazardous material into a cargo tank. What must you do before you move the vehicle>
qas.a: Close all manholes and valves. Be sure they are free of leaks

qas.q: A properly prepared uniform hazardous waste manifest:
qas.a: must be signed and carried by anyone transporting a hazardous waste

qas.q: In column 1, the + sign means:
qas.a: the proper shipping name (whether the material meets the definition of that class or not_

qas.q: When is it necessary to ground the trailer?
qas.a: When filling or unloading any flammable liquid tank. Ground must be maintained until filling hole is properly closed.

qas.q: When a spill occurs of an RQ substance the carrier has within ___ to report the spill
qas.a: 30 days

qas.q: Which of the following is a way to decide which placards to use?
qas.a: Materials hazard class, amount of hazardous materials being shipped, total weight of all classes of hazardous materials on your vehicle

qas.q: You may not park a vehicle carrying hazardous materials within ___ feet of an open fire
Who is responsible for certifying on the bills of lading the hazardous materials are prepared properly?
qas.a: the shipper

qas.q: You are hauling hazardous materials and you find that one of the tires is leaking. you must
qas.a: stop at the nearest safe place and fix it

qas.q: Who is responsible for checking to be sure the shipper correctly names, labeled, and marked hazardous materials shipment?
qas.a: The carrier and the driver

qas.q: If an "X" or "rq" is in the "hm" column of a shipping paper entry, the
qas.a: shipment is regulated by hazardous material regulations

qas.q: Who is responsible for finding out what permits or special routes you will need to haul hazardous materials?
qas.a: driver

qas.q: The shippers package hazardous materials, they must certify that this was done according to the regulations. The only exception is when:
qas.a: The shipper is a private carrier carrying his or her own product

qas.q: Which of the following, if required, must the shipper put on the package
qas.a: the required label, the hazardous materials shipping name and ID, the name and address of shipper

qas.q: A placard is required for a combustible liquid at
qas.a: more than 1001 pounds or more
